'''Justin Poore''' (born 26 January 1985) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer. A [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] [[State of Origin series|State of Origin]] representative forward, he played in Australia's [[NRL]] for the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] and [[Parramatta Eels]] before moving to England to play for the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Born in [[Perth]], Western Australia,<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.theleader.com.au/news/local/sport/rugby-league/justin-poore-playing-at-home/1432356.aspx |title=Justin Poore playing at 'home' – Local News – Sport – Rugby League – St George & Sutherland Shire Leader |access-date=2010-07-05 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110726010952/http://www.theleader.com.au/news/local/sport/rugby-league/justin-poore-playing-at-home/1432356.aspx |archive-date=26 July 2011 }}</ref> While attending Endeavour Sports High School in 2002, Poore was selected to play for the [[Australia national schoolboy rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys team]].<ref>{{cite web|publisher=Sporting Pulse|title=Australian Secondary Schools Rugby League|url=http://www.sportingpulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424|access-date=10 October 2008|archive-date=31 January 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170131030043/http://websites.sportstg.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424|url-status=dead}}</ref> Poore played his junior football for the Helensburgh Tigers before being signed by the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]].

==Professional playing career==
[[File:Justin Poore (2 August 2008).jpg|thumb|right|Poore warming up for the Dragons]]

===St. George Illawarra Dragons===
In round 1 of the [[2004 NRL season]] Poore made his premiership début for [[St. George Illawarra Dragons|St. George Illawarra]] against the [[Canberra Raiders]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.rleague.com//db/player/p/poore_justin/index.php |title=Archived copy |access-date=2012-10-09 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130116200726/http://rleague.com/db/player/p/poore_justin/index.php |archive-date=16 January 2013 }}</ref> In August 2008, Poore was named in the [[Australia national rugby league team#Rugby League World Cup Squad|46-man Kangaroos preliminary squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{cite news|author=Liam FitzGibbon |publisher=Fox Sports News (Australia) |title=Surprises in Kangaroos squad |url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,24111780-23214,00.html |access-date=2 August 2008 |date=1 August 2008 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080808144730/http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0%2C8659%2C24111780-23214%2C00.html |archive-date= 8 August 2008 }}</ref><ref name="Veteran Lockyer named in Australian squad">{{cite web|url=http://www.iht.com/articles/ap/2008/08/01/sports/AS-RGL-Australia-Squad.php|title=Veteran Lockyer named in Australian squad|publisher=International Herald Tribune|date=1 August 2008|access-date=2 August 2008}}</ref> After the [[2008 NRL season]], Poore and a group of [[NRL]] players led by former first-grader and ABC commentator [[Paul Osborne]] travelled to [[Rwanda]] on behalf of the Hope Rwanda charity, where they helped build 60 cement-rendered homes and a community hall.  Poore planned to raise more money for the charity and travel to Rwanda again after the 2009 season.<ref name="Poore's African effort a life-changing experience">{{cite web|url=http://www.illawarramercury.com.au/news/local/sport/rugby-league/poores-african-effort-a-lifechanging-experience/1417614.aspx|title=Poore's African effort a life-changing experience|publisher=Illawarra Mercury|date=28 January 2009|access-date=28 January 2009}}</ref>

In May 2009, Poore was selected for [[Country New South Wales rugby league team|Country]] in the annual [[City vs Country Origin|City vs Country]] match.<ref>{{cite news|work=[[Fox Sports (Australia)|Fox Sports]]|location=Australia|title=Blues halfback duel in two as Mitchell Pearce misses selection|first=Todd|last=Balym|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,25422326-5016947,00.html|archive-url=https://archive.today/20121205020103/http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,25422326-5016947,00.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=5 December 2012|date=3 May 2009|access-date=3 May 2009}}</ref> In May 2009, Poore was named to make his Origin début for [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] in the opening match of the [[2009 State of Origin series]] in [[Melbourne]], Victoria.<ref>{{cite news|last=Fitzgibbon|first=Liam|publisher=Fox Sports News (Australia)|title=NSW selectors go with form and not reputation for Origin opener|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,25537374-5018993,00.html|archive-url=https://archive.today/20120730123123/http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,25537374-5018993,00.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=30 July 2012|access-date=30 May 2009|date=25 May 2009}}</ref> He was one of only four New South Wales players who played in all 3 games of the 2009 series, the others being [[Jarryd Hayne]], [[Kurt Gidley]] and [[Ben Creagh]].

Poore made 21 appearances for St. George in his final season at the club as they won the 2009 [[Minor Premiership]] but crashed out of the finals after losing both games against Parramatta and the [[Brisbane Broncos]].  Poore played 128 games in total for St. George before signing a three-year contract with the [[Parramatta Eels]] starting in 2010.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.illawarramercury.com.au/news/local/sport/rugby-league/dragon-justin-poore-signs-with-parramatta-eels/1537223.aspx|title=Dragon Justin Poore signs with Parramatta Eels|first=Australian Community Media – Fairfax|last=Media|date=10 June 2009|website=Illawarramercury.com.au|access-date=25 August 2017}}</ref>

===Parramatta Eels===
At the start of the [[2010 NRL season]], Poore was one of Parramatta's high-profile recruits who was expected to bolster the club's forward pack.  Poore made a total of 23 appearances for the club in 2010 as they missed out on the finals after finishing a disappointing 12th.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/parramatta-eels-five-times-they-should-have-been-set-for-a-big-season-but-flopped/news-story/70bdcb6a002101a0640921d067eb4c49/|title=Parramatta Eels: Five times they should have been set for a big season but flopped|work=Fox Sports|date=20 March 2018 }}</ref>

Poore was forced to sit out for the majority of the [[2011 NRL season]] due to a knee injury. He returned in round 22 against [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]] which ended in a 56–6 loss at [[ANZ Stadium]].  Parramatta narrowly avoided the wooden spoon that year and defeated the [[Gold Coast Titans|Gold Coast]] in the final game of the season to avoid finishing last.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/parramatta-eels-defeat-the-gold-coast-titans-to-avoid-wooden-spoon-for-2011/news-story/fc87216a6a23a4b8d0eefd6174b60c47/|title=Parramatta Eels defeat the Gold Coast Titans to avoid wooden spoon for 2011|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=London}}</ref>

In the [[2012 NRL season]], Poore and Parramatta endured a horror season as the club finished last on the table for the first time since 1972.  At the end of 2012, Poore was released by the club.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/poore-just-happy-to-have-his-health-20120301-1u5wf.html/|title=Poore just happy to have his health|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=March 2012 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/kieran-forans-contract-dramas-at-parramatta-highlight-eels-long-history-of-recruitment-struggles/news-story/cc768fb720a2b396a5c7975751b53c84/|title=Kieran Foran's contract dramas at Parramatta highlight Eels' long history of recruitment struggles|work=Fox Sports|date=23 June 2015 }}</ref>

===Wakefield Trinity Wildcats===
In October 2012, Poore signed a two-year contract with the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] of the [[Super League]] starting in 2013.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.nrl.com/parramattas-poore-off-to-wakefield/tabid/10874/newsid/70270/default.aspx |title=News Article |access-date=2012-10-20 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121021210140/http://www.nrl.com/parramattas-poore-off-to-wakefield/tabid/10874/newsid/70270/default.aspx |archive-date=21 October 2012 }}</ref>

Poore signed a two-year deal with [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in October 2013.

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
Poore commenced playing with Hull KR in the [[2014 Super League season]]. He retired at the end of the season due to a knee injury.<ref>{{cite web|title=Hull KR's Australian forward Justin Poore announces retirement due to knee injury|url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12040/9533234/hull-krs-australian-forward-justin-poore-announces-retirement-due-to-knee-injury|publisher=Sky Sports|access-date=18 January 2015|date=25 October 2014}}</ref>
